title: Academic Figures MCP Architecture v2
goal: Create a strict repo-specific architecture diagram that matches the current codebase exactly: MCP hosts and VS Code extension at the edge, presentation and application stacked in the center, then a bottom row with domain on the left and infrastructure on the right, plus PubMed/providers/output artifacts on the integration side.
visual_direction: strict, technical, repo-specific, box-and-arrow, publication-safe architecture poster with explicit dependency direction

source_context.tagline: DDD academic figure MCP server and VS Code extension
source_context.summary: Presentation -> Application -> Domain <- Infrastructure, wired by dependency injection, with MCP hosts on the edge and provider/artifact integrations on the side

must_include: large readable labels for AI Hosts, VS Code Extension, MCP Server, presentation/, application/, domain/, infrastructure/, PubMed, Gemini/OpenRouter/Ollama, .academic-figures outputs
presentation above application in a central vertical stack
domain and infrastructure must appear side-by-side on the bottom row, with domain on the left and infrastructure on the right
an explicit arrow from infrastructure into domain to show Domain <- Infrastructure, not a vertical stack from domain down into infrastructure
an explicit legend or footer note showing Presentation → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure
clean arrows with no ambiguous loops and no unrelated external services
references: Only show integrations that actually exist in this repo: PubMed, Gemini/OpenRouter/Ollama, output artifacts, MCP hosts, VS Code extension
Do not show Crossref, OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, generic cloud APIs, or unrelated microservices

prompt_pack.prompt:
## Block 1: TITLE & PURPOSE
title: 'Academic Figures MCP Architecture v2'
purpose: Create a strict architecture diagram that matches the current repository, not a generic software diagram. It must explain the real DDD layering and the real integration edges of Academic Figures MCP.

## Block 2: LAYOUT
asset_kind: system_architecture_diagram_v2
figure_type: flowchart
composition: Wide architecture poster with three horizontal bands. Top band: AI hosts and VS Code extension. Middle band: MCP server wrapper containing presentation above application. Bottom band: two side-by-side blocks only, with domain on the left and infrastructure on the right. Domain and infrastructure must NOT be stacked vertically. Right edge shows integrations and outputs. Add one small bottom legend for dependency direction.

## Block 3: ELEMENTS
repo: academic-figures-mcp
exact_center_layers: presentation/ ; application/
exact_bottom_row: domain/ on the left ; infrastructure/ on the right
exact_edge_labels: AI Hosts ; VS Code Extension ; MCP Server ; PubMed ; Gemini/OpenRouter/Ollama ; .academic-figures outputs
layer_sublabels: presentation = server/tools/prompts/resources/dependencies ; application = plan/generate/edit/evaluate/batch use cases ; domain = classifier/entities/interfaces/value_objects/exceptions ; infrastructure = pubmed_client/gemini_adapter/prompt_engine/journal_registry/config
must_include: a clean MCP Server wrapper around presentation and application; a leftward arrow from infrastructure into domain; a downward arrow from application into domain; one output branch to generated PNG/SVG and jobs/evaluations artifacts
references: dependency direction must be visually obvious as Presentation -> Application -> Domain <- Infrastructure

## Block 4: COLOR
palette: deep academic navy frame/background, warm ivory layer cards, teal arrows for dependency flow, amber highlights for integrations and output artifacts
style_preset: repo_architecture_clean
visual_direction: strict, exact, technical, highly legible, polished repository poster

## Block 5: TEXT
language: English
text_usage: use only the exact labels above and short sublabels; keep all node text large, short, and readable; do not add unrelated services or extra examples

## Block 6: STYLE
style: polished technical architecture diagram, aligned boxes, clear arrow hierarchy, one small legend/footer, vector-like clarity
negative_constraints: do not stack domain over infrastructure or infrastructure under domain; avoid generic external API examples; avoid clutter; avoid tiny labels; avoid ambiguous arrows; avoid fake code screenshots; avoid photorealism

## Block 7: SIZE
canvas: 1536x1024
